```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(DEoptim)
library(lmomco)
library(DEoptim)
library(tidyr)
```






```{r}
# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

# Input and Output Directories
metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir    <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Create the Plots directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD REGIONAL MASTER DATA
# =====================================================================
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))

cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
```



```{r}
t2_val<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(Station_ID=="DE_00020")
k_seq<-t2_val$k_hours
t2_val<-t2_val$t2_pos
l1<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(Station_ID=="DE_00020")
l1<-l1$l1_pos
l2<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(Station_ID=="DE_00020")
l2<-l2$l2_pos
```




```{r}
library(DEoptim)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Define the Ratio of Power Laws Function
# f(k) = c + W * [ (k + d1)^-a / (k + d2)^-b ]
ratio_power_model <- function(k, alpha, beta, d1, d2, W, cc) {
  num <- (k + d1)^-alpha
  den <- (k + d2)^-beta
  return(cc + W * (num / den))
}

# 2. Filter data for k >= 24
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are already aligned in your environment
df_tail <- data.frame(k = k_seq, t2 = t2_val) %>%
  filter(k >= 24)

k_train <- df_tail$k
t2_train <- df_tail$t2

# 3. Objective Function
obj_func_tail <- function(params) {
  a   <- params[1]
  b   <- params[2]
  d1  <- params[3]
  d2  <- params[4]
  W   <- params[5]
  cc  <- params[6]
  
  preds <- ratio_power_model(k_train, a, b, d1, d2, W, cc)
  
  # Minimize SSE
  return(sum((t2_train - preds)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# 4. Optimization for the Tail
# We narrow the search for 'c' around your tail values (0.4 - 0.5)
set.seed(123)
fit_tail <- DEoptim(fn = obj_func_tail, 
                    lower = c(0.1, 0.1, 0.0, 0.0, 0.01, 0.35), 
                    upper = c(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 0.50), 
                    control = DEoptim.control(itermax = 300, trace = 50))

# 5. Extraction
res_t <- fit_tail$optim$bestmem
a_t <- res_t[1]; b_t <- res_t[2]
d1_t <- res_t[3]; d2_t <- res_t[4]
W_t <- res_t[5]; c_t <- res_t[6]

# 6. Visualization
k_smooth <- seq(24, 240, length.out = 200)
df_line <- data.frame(k = k_smooth) %>%
  mutate(y = ratio_power_model(k, a_t, b_t, d1_t, d2_t, W_t, c_t))

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = df_tail, aes(x = k, y = t2), size = 3, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = df_line, aes(x = k, y = y), color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Tail Fit (k >= 24): Ratio of Power Laws",
       subtitle = paste("Alpha:", round(a_t, 2), "| Beta:", round(b_t, 2), "| Limit C:", round(c_t, 4)),
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)") 
```

```{r}
# 1. Use the BEST parameters from your previous fit_tail optimization
# (Ensure these variables are in your environment from the previous step)
a_opt  <- res_t[1]
b_opt  <- res_t[2]
d1_opt <- res_t[3]
d2_opt <- res_t[4]
W_opt  <- res_t[5]
c_opt  <- res_t[6]

# 2. Generate predictions for ALL k (1 to 240)
k_all_seq <- seq(1, 240, length.out = 500)
df_validation <- data.frame(k = k_all_seq) %>%
  mutate(predicted = ratio_power_model(k, a_opt, b_opt, d1_opt, d2_opt, W_opt, c_opt))

# 3. Create a combined data frame for the original points
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are your original full vectors
df_original <- data.frame(k = k_seq, observed = t2_val) %>%
  mutate(region = ifelse(k >= 24, "Training (Tail)", "Validation (Head)"))

# 4. Plot the results
ggplot() +
  # The Full Prediction Line
  geom_line(data = df_validation, aes(x = k, y = predicted), 
            color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  # The Data Points (Colored by region)
  geom_point(data = df_original, aes(x = k, y = observed, color = region), size = 3) +
  # Vertical line at the training threshold
  geom_vline(xintercept = 24, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Training (Tail)" = "black", "Validation (Head)" = "orange")) +
  labs(title = "Model Validation: Tail-Optimized Parameters on All Data",
       subtitle = "Does the tail-fit predict the low-k hump?",
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)",
       color = "Data Region") +
  theme_minimal()
```






```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(DEoptim)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# =========================================================================
# 1. Define the Stretched Surge Model (Kohlrausch)
# =========================================================================
stretched_surge_model <- function(k, c, A, alpha, beta, gamma) {
  return(c + A * (k^alpha) * exp(-(beta * k)^gamma))
}

# =========================================================================
# 2. Define the Objective Function
# =========================================================================
obj_stretched <- function(p) {
  # p = c(c, A, alpha, beta, gamma)
  t2_est <- stretched_surge_model(k_seq, p[1], p[2], p[3], p[4], p[5])
  
  # Calculate Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)
  err_t2 <- mean(abs((t2_val - t2_est) / t2_val))
  
  # Optional: Add a slight penalty to keep gamma within physically realistic bounds 
  # (though DEoptim bounds usually handle this, a soft penalty helps stability)
  penalty <- ifelse(p[5] <= 0, 1e6, 0)
  
  return(err_t2 + penalty)
}

# =========================================================================
# 3. Optimization Setup
# =========================================================================
set.seed(42)

# Bounds for p = c(c, A, alpha, beta, gamma)
# gamma (p[5]): allow anything from 0.1 (very stretched) to 3.0 (very compressed)
lower_bounds <- c(0.2, 0.001, 0.01, 0.001, 0.1)
upper_bounds <- c(0.8, 10,    5,    1,     3.0)

# Run Optimizer
fit_stretched <- DEoptim(obj_stretched, 
                         lower = lower_bounds, 
                         upper = upper_bounds, 
                         control = list(itermax = 1500, trace = FALSE))

# =========================================================================
# 4. Extract and Print Results
# =========================================================================
opt_p <- fit_stretched$optim$bestmem
cat("\n=== Stretched Surge Model Parameters ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Baseline (c): %.4f\n", opt_p[1]))
cat(sprintf("Scale (A): %.4f\n", opt_p[2]))
cat(sprintf("Power Rise (alpha): %.4f\n", opt_p[3]))
cat(sprintf("Exp Decay (beta): %.4f\n", opt_p[4]))
cat(sprintf("Shape Parameter (gamma): %.4f\n", opt_p[5]))

if (opt_p[5] > 1) {
  cat("Result: gamma > 1 (Compressed exponential: sharper drop-off)\n")
} else if (opt_p[5] < 1) {
  cat("Result: gamma < 1 (Stretched exponential: longer, dragging tail)\n")
}

# =========================================================================
# 5. Visualization
# =========================================================================
k_smooth <- exp(seq(log(min(k_seq)), log(max(k_seq)), length.out = 500))
t2_pred_smooth <- stretched_surge_model(k_smooth, opt_p[1], opt_p[2], opt_p[3], opt_p[4], opt_p[5])

df_pred <- data.frame(k = k_smooth, t2 = t2_pred_smooth)
df_orig <- data.frame(k = k_seq, t2 = t2_val) %>%
  mutate(Scale = ifelse(k >= 24, "Tail (>= 24h)", "Head (< 24h)"))

ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = df_pred, aes(x = k, y = t2), color = "#27ae60", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = df_orig, aes(x = k, y = t2, fill = Scale), size = 3, shape = 21, color="black") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Head (< 24h)" = "#f39c12", "Tail (>= 24h)" = "#2c3e50")) +
  labs(title = "Stretched Surge Model Fit (Kohlrausch)",
       subtitle = sprintf("gamma = %.3f | beta = %.4f", opt_p[5], opt_p[4]),
       x = "Temporal scale, k [h] (Log Scale)", 
       y = expression(t[2])) +
  theme_minimal()
```







```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(DEoptim)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)


# =========================================================================
# 1. SETUP TRAIN & TEST STATIONS
# =========================================================================
all_stations <- unique(master_regional_data$Final_Summary$Station_ID)

# Randomly pick 5 stations for training
train_stations <- sample(all_stations, 100)

# Pick 1 test station (not in training set)
test_station <- sample(setdiff(all_stations, train_stations), 1)

cat("Training Stations:", paste(train_stations, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Test Station:", test_station, "\n\n")

# =========================================================================
# 2. TRAINING PHASE: BUILD params_df & FIND THE REGIONAL RATIO
# =========================================================================
surge_4param <- function(k, A, alpha, beta, gamma) {
  return(A * (k^alpha) * exp(-(beta * k)^gamma))
}

# Initialize an empty list to store the dataframes for each station
results_list <- list()

for (i in seq_along(train_stations)) {
  stn <- train_stations[i]
  stn_data <- master_regional_data$Final_Summary %>% filter(Station_ID == stn)
  
  obj_train <- function(p) {
    est <- surge_4param(stn_data$k_hours, p[1], p[2], p[3], p[4])
    return(mean(abs((stn_data$t2_pos - est) / stn_data$t2_pos))) # MAPE
  }
  
  # Fit full 4-parameter model
  fit <- suppressWarnings(DEoptim(obj_train, lower = c(0.1, 0.01, 0.001, 0.1), 
                                  upper = c(10, 5, 1, 3), 
                                  control = list(itermax = 400, trace = FALSE)))
  
  opt_p <- fit$optim$bestmem
  
  # Store the results for this specific station in a dataframe
  results_list[[i]] <- data.frame(
    Station_ID = stn,
    A_param = opt_p[1],
    alpha_param = opt_p[2],
    beta_param = opt_p[3],
    gamma_param = opt_p[4],
    Ratio_A_alpha = opt_p[1] / opt_p[2], # Calculate ratio directly here
    MAPE = fit$optim$bestval
  )
}

# Combine the list of dataframes into a single params_df
params_df <- bind_rows(results_list)

# Calculate the mean ratio from the dataframe
R_mean <- mean(params_df$Ratio_A_alpha)

cat(sprintf("=== Regional Parameter Link ===\nMean Ratio (A / alpha) = %.4f\n\n", R_mean))

# View the first few rows of your new dataframe
head(params_df)

# =========================================================================
# 3. TESTING PHASE: FIT TAIL OF UNSEEN STATION USING LINKED MODEL
# =========================================================================
test_data <- master_regional_data$Final_Summary %>% filter(Station_ID == test_station)
tail_data <- test_data %>% filter(k_hours >= 24)

# 3-Parameter Linked Model (A is calculated dynamically as R_mean * alpha)
surge_linked <- function(k, alpha, beta, gamma) {
  linked_A <- R_mean * alpha
  return(linked_A * (k^alpha) * exp(-(beta * k)^gamma))
}

obj_test_tail <- function(p) {
  # p = c(alpha, beta, gamma)
  est <- surge_linked(tail_data$k_hours, p[1], p[2], p[3])
  return(mean(abs((tail_data$t2_pos - est) / tail_data$t2_pos)))
}

# Fit ONLY to the tail of the test station
fit_test <- suppressWarnings(DEoptim(obj_test_tail, lower = c(0.01, 0.001, 0.1), upper = c(5, 1, 3), 
                                     control = list(itermax = 500, trace = FALSE)))

opt_p <- fit_test$optim$bestmem
predicted_A <- R_mean * opt_p[1]

cat("=== Test Station Parameters (Fitted on Tail Only) ===\n")
cat(sprintf("alpha (Fitted): %.4f\n", opt_p[1]))
cat(sprintf("A (Predicted via Ratio): %.4f\n", predicted_A))
cat(sprintf("beta (Fitted): %.4f\n", opt_p[2]))
cat(sprintf("gamma (Fitted): %.4f\n", opt_p[3]))

# =========================================================================
# 4. EXTRAPOLATE AND VISUALIZE
# =========================================================================
k_smooth <- exp(seq(log(min(test_data$k_hours)), log(max(test_data$k_hours)), length.out = 500))
t2_pred_smooth <- surge_linked(k_smooth, opt_p[1], opt_p[2], opt_p[3])

df_pred <- data.frame(k = k_smooth, t2 = t2_pred_smooth)
df_orig <- test_data %>%
  mutate(Scale = ifelse(k_hours >= 24, "Tail (>= 24h) [Fitted]", "Head (< 24h) [Hidden/Extrapolated]"))

ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = df_pred, aes(x = k, y = t2), color = "#d35400", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = df_orig, aes(x = k_hours, y = t2_pos, fill = Scale), size = 3, shape = 21, color="black") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Head (< 24h) [Hidden/Extrapolated]" = "orange", "Tail (>= 24h) [Fitted]" = "midnightblue")) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Ratio Cross-Validation: Station %s", test_station),
       subtitle = sprintf("Trained entirely on tail data | Linked Ratio A/alpha = %.2f", R_mean),
       x = "Temporal scale, k [h] (Log Scale)", 
       y = expression(t[2])) +
  theme_minimal()



```


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork) # For arranging multiple plots easily

# =========================================================================
# 1. MERGE DATA AND CALCULATE THE RATIO
# =========================================================================
# We take the parameters you already optimized and join them with the metadata
analysis_df <- params_df %>%
  left_join(master_regional_data[["Quality_Metadata"]], by = "Station_ID") %>%
  # Calculate our target ratio
  mutate(Ratio_A_alpha = A_param / alpha_param)

# Check to ensure merge was successful and drop NAs if any metadata is missing
analysis_df <- analysis_df %>% filter(!is.na(Ratio_A_alpha), !is.na(Elevation_m))

# =========================================================================
# 2. GENERATE SCATTER PLOTS (X, Y, Z)
# =========================================================================
# Plot 1: Ratio vs Longitude (X)
p_x <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Longitude, y = Ratio_A_alpha)) +
  geom_point(color = "#3498db", size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Ratio vs. Longitude (X)", 
       x = "Longitude", 
       y = expression(Ratio~(A/alpha))) +
  theme_bw()

# Plot 2: Ratio vs Latitude (Y)
p_y <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Latitude, y = Ratio_A_alpha)) +
  geom_point(color = "#2ecc71", size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Ratio vs. Latitude (Y)", 
       x = "Latitude", 
       y = expression(Ratio~(A/alpha))) +
  theme_bw()

# Plot 3: Ratio vs Elevation (Z)
p_z <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Elevation_m, y = Ratio_A_alpha)) +
  geom_point(color = "#e67e22", size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Ratio vs. Elevation (Z)", 
       x = "Elevation (m)", 
       y = expression(Ratio~(A/alpha))) +
  theme_bw()

# =========================================================================
# 3. GENERATE SPATIAL MAP (X vs Y colored by Ratio)
# =========================================================================
p_map <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Ratio_A_alpha)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "A / alpha") +
  labs(title = "Spatial Distribution of the Ratio", 
       subtitle = "Looking for geographic clustering",
       x = "Longitude", 
       y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# =========================================================================
# 4. DISPLAY ALL PLOTS TOGETHER
# =========================================================================
# The patchwork package makes this syntax beautifully simple
(p_x | p_y | p_z) / p_map
```
